title: Tragacantha tephrosioides
taxonomy: Tragacantha tephrosioides is classified within the family Fabaceae, which is also known as the legume family. This classification reflects the plant's characteristics, such as its compound leaves and pea-like flowers. Tragacantha tephrosioides is further classified within the genus Tragacantha, which is a group of plants that are commonly known as hair sages or sainfoins. The exact species classification of Tragacantha tephrosioides may vary depending on the source, but it is generally considered to be a species of shrub or perennial plant. Further classification within the kingdom and other taxonomic ranks is also available but is more specific.
uses: Tragacantha tephrosioides, also known as the Egyptian fenugreek, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. Its leaves and seeds contain flavonoids and other compounds that have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
